content: Ari flagged commit chain backlog hit 8K during Tuesday's write spike. Queue backed up across memory layer. Looked like cascade from Anthropic integration batch.
summary: Commit backlog reached 8K on Apr 1 write spike

content: Merkle root batching strategy: group pending commits by hash tree, reduce individual queue operations by ~60%. Ari's approach clusters small writes into atomic blocks. Reduces DB contention significantly.
summary: Merkle root batching reduces commit queue pressure by 60%

content: When commit backlog exceeds 5K: check DataDog memory layer metrics (p99 latency, Sentry error rate), then page Ari. If > 10K, trigger incident protocol. Document root cause in Linear ticket before resolving.
Wednesday morning standup: Devon and James both suggested tuning Snowflake write buffer from 2s to 5s intervals. Ari worried about latency trade-off. Decided to test on staging first.
summary: Devon & James proposed Snowflake buffer tuning (2s→5s)

content: RCA template for queue incidents: 1) timeline of backlog growth, 2) write volume spike correlation, 3) commit success/failure rates, 4) downstream impact on Acme/Initech, 5) remediation applied, 6) prevention next steps.
summary: Incident RCA structure for commit queue events

content: High write volume (>2K ops/min) triggers cascade risk in memory layer. Batch window becomes critical—too narrow risks queue overflow, too wide (>10s) affects agent latency SLA. Sweet spot appears ~5s.
summary: Memory layer cascade risk at 2K+ ops/min write rate

content: Monitor commit chain depth weekly via DataDog dashboard (memory.commit_chain_depth metric). Alert if exceeds 12K. Share trend snapshot with Sebastien monthly for capacity planning.
summary: Weekly commit chain depth monitoring + monthly capacity review

content: I've defaulted to waiting for Ari to surface technical escalations, but queue incidents need earlier detection. Need to own DataDog dashboard checks 3x daily during high-traffic windows. Can't offload this.
summary: Need proactive queue monitoring, not reactive escalations

content: Postmortem on Apr 1 incident: Ari's merkle root batching deployed Thursday night. Backlog dropped from 8.2K to 1.8K by end of day. No customer-facing latency reported, but Initech's async job queue delayed ~90s.
summary: Merkle batching deployment cleared 8K backlog by Apr 10

content: Audit findings: commit chain integrity depends on three layers—Linear issue tracking for tickets, Notion doc for decision history, GitHub commits for code. Missing any layer creates blind spot in RCA.
summary: Three-layer audit requirement for commit chain integrity

content: Post-incident checklist: 1) Update Linear ticket status, 2) post postmortem summary to #incidents Slack channel within 24h, 3) assign prevention owner, 4) schedule follow-up audit with Anya (Finance) for cost impact review.
Realized I've been documenting decisions async but not forcing async review cycles on Ari/Devon. Need to set 18h SLA for technical decision sign-offs to avoid cascading delays during crises.
summary: Must enforce async decision review SLAs with eng team
